microsoft_ft: Served as the **technical bridge** between Azure's engineering teams and enterprise partners (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp), translating complex integration requirements into actionable deployment plans and sustaining 45+ annual resilience drills across production environments.

Led **0→1 deployment** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining self-service scheduling requirements and ETL workflows that cut operational toil by 30% and enabled partner teams to execute integrations without manual hand-holding.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering a full deployment in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** — surfacing critical hardware defects before they impacted customers and establishing a repeatable validation model.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, acting as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation deployment tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, coordinating across engineering, operations, and partner stakeholders in real time.

Built an internal **AI-powered planning agent** that restructured drill-scheduling workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing deployment capacity by 21% — directly expanding the team's ability to serve more agency-scale partners.

Scaled a 2-person recovery validation operation into a platformized, self-service system driving **$14M+ in business impact**, standardizing integration workflows so new partners could onboard faster and field teams could resolve deployment issues independently.
microsoft_2023: Championed **production adoption** of AI-driven code generation workflows across customer-facing Azure service teams, running live demos and hands-on training sessions that drove utilization across 14 teams and freed 37 engineering hours monthly for higher-value deployment work.

Surfaced critical product gaps by conducting **11+ structured user interviews** with Azure service teams, directly influencing the roadmap to prioritize intent-based YAML generation — mirroring the kind of field discovery that turns deployments into long-term partnerships.

Improved data integrity and retrieval across internal documentation by migrating to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool with rigorous metadata standards, cutting lookup time by 83% and enabling engineers to self-serve answers during live integration sessions.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework — coordinating across 140+ teams with competing priorities to unblock critical deployment paths.

Conducted **deployment discovery** with 20+ service teams to surface 81 hours of per-region manual effort, then built a phased roadmap to automate critical paths — exactly the kind of on-the-ground problem-solving that turns complex customer environments into scalable systems.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, giving leadership real-time visibility into integration bottlenecks and enabling data-driven prioritization of the highest-impact automation investments across the deployment pipeline.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot by owning the legacy OS migration deployment strategy end-to-end — mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations and coordinating with IT, Operations, and Engineering to resolve integration blockers before go-live.

Drove cross-functional alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing the manual integration overhead that slows customer-facing rollouts.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies and owned backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority integration tickets** and stabilizing the developer intake process — building the kind of cross-team coordination muscle that forward-deployed roles demand daily.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by deploying a new CRM-based workflow to manage end-to-end scoping and invoicing for 200+ monthly proposals — translating a messy, manual customer process into a structured, repeatable system that scaled without adding headcount.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to reach customers in financial difficulty seeking home services — an audience that demands both empathy and clarity.

Identified and sealed margin leaks by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, surfacing pricing model gaps and delivering actionable recommendations that improved leadership's ability to make data-driven decisions on program pricing.

tailoring_notes: 'Bridge' framing from JD ('Be the bridge') woven into microsoft_ft bullet 1 as 'technical bridge between Azure's engineering teams and enterprise partners' — mirrors Promise's core FDE value proposition.

'Deployment' and 'integration' vocabulary from JD used throughout all roles: microsoft_ft bullets 1–3 reference 'deployment plans,' 'integration requirements,' and 'ETL workflows'; amazon_robotics bullet 1 uses 'deployment strategy' and 'integration blockers.'

'On-the-ground problem-solving' and 'customer environments' framing from JD ('Problem-solve in the wild,' 'Dive into customer environments') embedded in microsoft_2022 bullet 2 explicitly and amazon_robotics bullet 1 contextually.

'Self-service' and 'partner onboarding' language from JD ('self-service scheduling,' long-term partnership') threaded into microsoft_ft bullets 2 and 6 to signal Cyrus understands Promise's deployment-to-partnership arc.

'AI-powered eligibility verification' and 'agency-scale' context from JD's Role Impact section reflected in microsoft_ft bullet 5, framing the AI planning agent as expanding capacity to serve 'agency-scale partners' — directly connecting to Promise's government/utility customer base.
